notes.hypothesis: Watering P01 from its dry baseline with a slow distributed top-water dose should produce a measurable wetting response across all four probes. Channel timing and final values may differ because top-water paths, leaf blockage, surface coverage, probe placement, and pot drainage create local wetting differences.
notes.method: P01 was kept in its inner nursery pot with drainage holes, sitting on a plate with a paper towel; the outer decorative pot was not used. Capture ran for 900 s at 1 s cadence from the dry baseline state. A kitchen drinking cup filled to about the 1/3-full mark was poured slowly over the exposed soil surface, moving around the pot rather than concentrating in one spot. The full prepared cup amount was used. No visible runoff or leak appeared from the pot base, and the paper towel remained completely dry. Probes were left in place during watering.
notes.findings: All channels responded quickly to watering. Final medians were s1 1230, s2 1458, s3 1146, s4 1065 raw ADC, compared with pre-water dry-baseline medians of s1 2110, s2 2164, s3 2189, s4 2098. The large raw decreases support a real wetting response: s1 -865, s2 -708, s3 -1025, s4 -999 counts from first to last sample. First-threshold crossings show fast local wetting: s3 and s4 dropped below 1400 within about 9-13 s of capture start; s1 dropped below 1400 at about 23 s; s2 did not reach 1400 during the run and crossed 1500 at about 120 s. The s1 trace showed multiple discrete wetting steps that aligned with the observed watering sequence: initial slow pour, pause/inspection, additional pour, then a final small pour near the s1 edge/dryish spot. s2 stayed relatively higher/drier than the others; visual inspection showed a leaf may have partially blocked water from reaching the surface closest to s2, and that area appeared possibly drier. No runoff was observed.
notes.conclusion: This run should be treated as a dry-to-watered/no-runoff transition, not a first-leak endpoint. P01 did not behave like one uniform soil volume: individual channels responded according to where water reached first and how it moved through the pot. The absence of runoff means the drainage threshold was not observed; the dose may be near a useful upper watering amount for this pot, but that remains an inference. Probe placement, leaf obstruction, and watering coverage materially affected the first 5-10 min of readings. Band labels remain provisional until per-channel calibration is ratified.
